questions.question: What is the primary benefit of establishing a 'Monday Review & Plan' rhythm?

options.text: To spend as many hours as possible on marketing.
To review last week's data and set three specific, non-negotiable marketing tasks for the week.
To reply to every single email in your inbox.
To buy new marketing tools every week.

questions.explanation: The Monday session ensures you are making data-driven decisions and focusing on the activities that move the needle.

questions.question: Which task is recommended as part of a 'Monthly Maintenance Checklist'?

options.text: Testing all your lead capture forms by submitting them yourself.
Redesigning your entire website from scratch.
Changing your company name.
Deleting your entire email list.

questions.explanation: Testing your forms monthly ensures that your capture engine is still working and data is flowing correctly into your CRM.

questions.question: What should a founder do during a 'Quarterly Strategic Deep-Dive'?

options.text: Look at daily traffic spikes only.
Assess long-term trends in conversion rates, evaluate channel performance, and audit the tool stack.
Hire 10 new employees.
Ignore the data and trust their gut feeling for the whole year.

questions.explanation: Quarterly reviews are for high-level strategy, ensuring the overall direction and unit economics (like LTV:CAC) are healthy.

questions.question: According to the lesson, how often should a solo founder typically check their marketing metrics to avoid 'Over-Monitoring'?

options.text: Every hour.
Ten times a day.
Once a week (with a quick mid-week check).
Once every six months.

questions.explanation: Weekly checks provide enough data to see trends without becoming a distraction from execution.

questions.question: What is the 'Set and Forget Fallacy'?

options.text: The belief that if you set a goal, you will automatically reach it.
The belief that automations and systems will work indefinitely without testing or maintenance.
Forgetting to set an alarm for your review sessions.
The idea that marketing is a one-time setup.

questions.explanation: Systems decay over time—links break, APIs change, and content gets stale. Regular maintenance is required.

questions.question: Establish your initial Marketing Operations rhythm. Which day and time will you commit to for your half-hour Monday Review? List three specific items from the monthly maintenance checklist that are most critical for your current system. How will you remind yourself to do them?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's commitment to a rhythm. Check if their chosen critical items match their current stack complexity. Suggest one 'Experimental' task they could add to their first quarterly review.
